([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Донецк, IPA: [[dɐˈnʲetsk]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Russian "Help:IPA/Russian") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Ru-%D0%94%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B5%D1%86%D0%BA.ogg "File:Ru-Донецк.ogg"), until 1955 **Gundorovka**, Russian: Гундоровка, [Ukrainian](/wiki/Ukrainian_language "Ukrainian language"): Гундорівка) is a [town](/wiki/Types_of_inhabited_localities_in_Russia "Types of inhabited localities in Russia") in [Rostov Oblast](/wiki/Rostov_Oblast "Rostov Oblast"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), located on the [Seversky Donets River](/wiki/Seversky_Donets_River "Seversky Donets River") on the border with [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"). By road it is located 388 kilometres (241 mi) west of [Volgograd](/wiki/Volgograd "Volgograd"). Population: 50,098 ([2010 census](/wiki/2010_Russian_census "2010 Russian census"));[[2]](#cite_note-2010Census-2) 48,040 ([2002 census](/wiki/2002_Russian_census "2002 Russian census"));[[6]](#cite_note-PopCensus-6) 48,673 ([1989 Soviet census](/wiki/1989_Soviet_census "1989 Soviet census"));[[7]](#cite_note-Census1989-7) 38,000 (1970).

["Об исчислении времени"](http://pravo.gov.ru/proxy/ips/?docbody=&prevDoc=102483854&backlink=1&&nd=102148085). *Официальный интернет-портал правовой информации* (in Russian). June 3, 2011. Retrieved January 19, 2019.
Почта России. Информационно-вычислительный центр ОАСУ РПО. (*Russian Post*). [Поиск объектов почтовой связи](http://vinfo.russianpost.ru/servlet/department) (*Postal Objects Search*) (in Russian)

[Federal State Statistics Service](/wiki/Federal_State_Statistics_Service_(Russia) "Federal State Statistics Service (Russia)") (May 21, 2004). Всероссийская перепись населения 2002 года [All-Russia Population Census of 2002] (in Russian).

Всесоюзная перепись населения 1989 года [All-Union Population Census of 1989] (in Russian). **Donetsk** ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Донецк, IPA: [[dɐˈnʲetsk]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Russian "Help:IPA/Russian") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Ru-%D0%94%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B5%D1%86%D0%BA.ogg "File:Ru-Донецк.ogg"), until 1955 **Gundorovka**, Russian: Гундоровка, [Ukrainian](/wiki/Ukrainian_language "Ukrainian language"): Гундорівка) is a [town](/wiki/Types_of_inhabited_localities_in_Russia "Types of inhabited localities in Russia") in [Rostov Oblast](/wiki/Rostov_Oblast "Rostov Oblast"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), located on the [Seversky Donets River](/wiki/Seversky_Donets_River "Seversky Donets River") on the border with [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"). By road it is located 388 kilometres (241 mi) west of [Volgograd](/wiki/Volgograd "Volgograd"). Population: 50,098 ([2010 census](/wiki/2010_Russian_census "2010 Russian census"));[[2]](#cite_note-2010Census-2) 48,040 ([2002 census](/wiki/2002_Russian_census "2002 Russian census"));[[6]](#cite_note-PopCensus-6) 48,673 ([1989 Soviet census](/wiki/1989_Soviet_census "1989 Soviet census"));[[7]](#cite_note-Census1989-7) 38,000 (1970).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk,_Rostov_Oblast&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/%D0%9E%D0%92%D0%94-%D0%A1%D1%82%D0%B0%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%86%D0%B0_%D0%93%D1%83%D0%BD%D0%B4%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B2%D1%81%D0%BA%D0%B0%D1%8F_%D0%A3%D1%81%D0%BF%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%81%D0%BA%D0%B0%D1%8F_%D1%86%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BA%D0%BE%D0%B2%D1%8C.jpg/250px-%D0%9E%D0%92%D0%94-%D0%A1%D1%82%D0%B0%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%86%D0%B0_%D0%93%D1%83%D0%BD%D0%B4%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B2%D1%81%D0%BA%D0%B0%D1%8F_%D0%A3%D1%81%D0%BF%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%81%D0%BA%D0%B0%D1%8F_%D1%86%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BA%D0%BE%D0%B2%D1%8C.jpg)](/wiki/File:%D0%9E%D0%92%D0%94-%D0%A1%D1%82%D0%B0%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%86%D0%B0_%D0%93%D1%83%D0%BD%D0%B4%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B2%D1%81%D0%BA%D0%B0%D1%8F_%D0%A3%D1%81%D0%BF%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%81%D0%BA%D0%B0%D1%8F_%D1%86%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BA%D0%BE%D0%B2%D1%8C.jpg)

Church of the Dormition of the Theotokos in the early 20th century

The settlement was founded in 1681 by the [Don Cossacks](/wiki/Don_Cossacks "Don Cossacks"). It was controlled by various factions between 1918 and 1924, i.e. the [Don Soviet Republic](/wiki/Don_Soviet_Republic "Don Soviet Republic"), the [Don Republic](/wiki/Don_Republic "Don Republic"), [Soviet Russia](/wiki/Soviet_Russia "Soviet Russia") and [Soviet Ukraine](/wiki/Soviet_Ukraine "Soviet Ukraine"). During [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), it was occupied by Germany in 1942–1943.

It was known as Gundorovka until 1955.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] A museum called the [Donetsk Museum of History and Ethnography](/wiki/Donetsk_Museum_of_History_and_Ethnography "Donetsk Museum of History and Ethnography") was built in the city in 2005.

## Administrative status

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk,_Rostov_Oblast&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Administrative status")]

Within the [framework of administrative divisions](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Russia#Administrative_divisions "Subdivisions of Russia"), it is incorporated as **Donetsk [Urban Okrug](/wiki/City_of_federal_subject_significance "City of federal subject significance")**—an administrative unit with the status equal to that of the [districts](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Rostov_Oblast "Administrative divisions of Rostov Oblast").[[1]](#cite_note-Ref133-1) As a [municipal division](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Russia#Municipal_divisions "Subdivisions of Russia"), this administrative unit also has urban okrug status.[[3]](#cite_note-Ref866-3)
